pi_items.source: Task specification - user-created process name for scenario modeling
Verified via standard energy units for electricity generation. kWh is the correct unit for electrical energy output.
Task specification - defined in metadata and description. Represents 40% of 1 kWh output from wind onshore generation.
Task specification - defined in metadata and description. Represents 30% of 1 kWh output from solar (PV) generation.
Task specification - defined in metadata and description. Represents 20% of 1 kWh output from nuclear generation.
Task specification - defined in metadata and description. Represents 10% of 1 kWh output from natural gas generation.
Verified via multiple sources: inchcalculator.com and unitjuggler.com confirm the exact conversion factor.

pi_items.verified_value: USLCI (US Life Cycle Inventory Database) from NREL
pi_items.source: Verified via NREL website and Federal LCA Commons. USLCI is a real, publicly available database containing US-specific lifecycle inventory data for electricity generation processes including wind, solar, nuclear, and natural gas.

pi_items.verified_value: TRACI 2.1 (Tool for the Reduction and Assessment of Chemical and other environmental Impacts 2.1)
pi_items.source: Verified via EPA and openLCA documentation. TRACI 2.1 is the US EPA's official Life Cycle Impact Assessment method, including Global Warming Potential (GWP) characterization factors. Last updated in 2012.
Verified via EPA and openLCA documentation. GWP is a core impact category in TRACI 2.1, measured in kg CO2-equivalents.
Task specification - expected output file path from export_result.sh script
Task specification - expected documentation file path from task description

privileged_info_summary: The task specifies a synthetic electricity grid scenario with 40% wind onshore, 30% solar PV, 20% nuclear, and 10% natural gas generation, totaling 1 kWh of output. The energy conversion factor of 1 kWh = 3.6 MJ is correct. The task uses verified, real databases: the USLCI (US Life Cycle Inventory) database from NREL containing US-specific electricity generation processes, and TRACI 2.1, the EPA's official Life Cycle Impact Assessment method for calculating Global Warming Potential impacts. Results are exported to /home/ga/LCA_Results/grid_2035_results.csv with process composition documented in /home/ga/LCA_Results/mix_composition.txt.
